title: thymus serpyllum var. prostratus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. prostratus, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum var. prostratus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. Thymus serpyllum var. prostratus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical application, and is also used in cooking and as a natural insect repellent.
